uses: Aeschrion excelsa, also known as the Golden Aesclepias or Yellow Milkweed, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems contain compounds with potential antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and efficacy. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Further studies are necessary to confirm its safety and effectiveness for human use.
properties: Aeschrion excelsa is not a widely recognized plant species. However, Aeschrion excelsa is sometimes considered to be the same plant as the "Aesculus" genus.  Aesculus species, including Aesculus hippocastanum and Aesculus glabra, have been used in traditional medicine for their potential therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bark of Aesculus species contains a compound called aesculin, which has been used to treat various conditions such as fever and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Aeschrion excelsa, if it exists as a separate species.
